_comment: Single source of truth consumed by .github/workflows/coverage.yml AND tools/scripts/local_diff_cover.sh. To change the threshold, compare branch, or per-file exclusions, edit here once. CONSUMED KEYS (each must be referenced from local_diff_cover.sh or coverage.yml — enforced by test_local_diff_cover.py::ConfigKeysAreConsumed): `diff_coverage_fail_under`, `compare_branch`, `diff_cover_excludes`. Source-set filtering (which files llvm-cov accumulates coverage for) lives in local_diff_cover.sh + scripts/run_coverage.sh's COVERAGE_IGNORE_REGEX, NOT in this JSON — adding `filters` / `exclude_filters` arrays here is a silent no-op (#1052) because nothing reads them. `diff_cover_excludes` is the line-coverage-exclude list passed to `diff-cover --exclude=...` — use sparingly for thin dispatchers / generated code that is exercised end-to-end via shell-out tests but doesn't propagate llvm-cov instrumentation. cmd_loop.cpp's watch-loop entry path (lines ~280-306) cannot be exercised by a shellout test (the loop blocks until SIGINT), so the file is exercised end-to-end via the --no-watch shellout tests in test/test_cli_shellout.cpp instead. cmd_coverage.cpp was previously excluded for the same reason but is now properly counted: local_diff_cover.sh now mirrors run_coverage.sh's wider object-discovery pattern (build/tools, build/inspect, build/bindings) so CLI shell-out tests propagate llvm-cov instrumentation through pulp-cli / pulp-standalone / pulp-inspect (#919 follow-up).
